name: Google Admin: Add Domain-Wide Delegation
description: Automates adding a service account client ID to Google Admin Console Domain-Wide Delegation. Uses Patchright to bypass bot detection.

client_id.description: The service account numeric Client ID (OAuth 2.0 Client ID)

steps.description: Get element refs — look for 'Add new' button in snapshot output
Click the 'Add new' button. Replace {{add_new_ref}} with the ref from step 3 snapshot that corresponds to the 'Add new' button.
steps.runtime_note: Run snapshot in step 3 first; use the ref for the element with name='Add new' or text='Add new'

steps.description: Fill the Client ID field with the service account numeric ID
steps.runtime_note: After clicking 'Add new', run snapshot to get the modal field refs. Use the ref for the Client ID input.

steps.description: Fill the OAuth scopes field with comma-separated scope URLs
steps.runtime_note: Use the ref for the OAuth Scopes textarea from the snapshot after 'Add new' modal opened

steps.description: Click the Authorize button to save the delegation entry
steps.runtime_note: Use the ref for the 'Authorize' button from the modal snapshot

steps.description: Take snapshot of the DWD table to verify {{client_id}} row now appears
Tear down Patchright browser session cleanly

steps.message: Domain-Wide Delegation entry added successfully. Client ID {{client_id}} is now authorized for scopes: {{oauth_scopes}}. Verify in Google Admin Console at https://admin.google.com/ac/owl/domainwidedelegation
steps.description: Confirm success to user with summary

rollback.description: Manual removal via Google Admin Console
steps: Find the row with the client_id used during authorization

known_issues.issue: Session older than 24h may require re-auth
known_issues.resolution: Re-copy Chrome profile: cp -r ~/Library/Application\ Support/Google/Chrome/Default ~/.eng-buddy/browser-profile/ (with Chrome closed), then restart dashboard, then retry

known_issues.symptoms: Browser fails to launch with lock error
